highlights.title: AI K-Pop Startup Galaxy Aims for IPO in Seoul, New York
highlights.description: Humanoid robots dressed in black, luxury hip-hop outfits snap into formation, moving in perfect sync to K-pop songs. It’s a simulation inside an office styled as a crash-landed spacecraft in the heart of Seoul’s financial district, complete with towering astronauts and floating robotic jellyfish.

highlights.title: Attacking bitcoin mining with a quantum computer would require the energy of a star, academics say
highlights.description: One paper finds that attacking the bitcoin blockchain through quantum mining would demand the energy output of a star. Another replicates every major "quantum factoring breakthrough" using a 1981 home computer and a dog.

highlights.title: Bain Data Center Unit Cuts Ties With Nvidia Buyer After US Probe
highlights.description: Bain Capital’s Bridge Data Centres has removed from its Malaysian computing hub a Southeast Asian company the US suspects of smuggling Nvidia Corp. chips.

highlights.title: Google quietly launched an AI dictation app that works offline
highlights.description: Google's new offline-first dictation app uses Gemma AI models to take on the apps like Wispr Flow.

highlights.title: 9to5Mac Overtime 066: Every time I breathe there’s a new Claude update
highlights.description:
Jeff unpacks his surprising journey from AI skeptic to enthusiast, sharing how he now uses tools like Claude Code to build custom solutions. Fernando provides an honest opinion about the new AirPods Max versus AirPods Pro 3, and a one-month check-in on the MacBook Neo.
9to5Mac Overtime is a weekly video-first podcast exploring fun and interesting observations in the Apple ecosystem, featuring 9to5Mac’s Fernando Silva & Jeff Benjamin. Subscribe to Overtime via Apple Podcasts and our YouTube channel for more.
more…

highlights.title: Zhipu Hikes Prices Again as China AI Monetization Wave Quickens
highlights.description: Zhipu raised the cost of access to its most advanced AI model by at least 8%, joining other leading Chinese artificial intelligence players in trying to profit off years of research and computing investments.

highlights.title: Robot Maker Kuka Eyes US, Asia as Europe’s Factories Lag on AI
highlights.description: Many of Europe’s industrial companies are too slow to adopt artificial intelligence, putting faster-moving global rivals in a position to overtake them, according to German-Chinese robotics maker Kuka AG.

highlights.title: Microsoft hints at bit bunkers for war zones
Men Are Buying Hacking Tools to Use Against Their Wives and Friends
highlights.description: In Telegram groups, men are sharing thousands of nonconsensual images of women and girls, buying spyware, and engaging in doxing and sexual abuse.

highlights.title: S. Korea Posts Record Current Account Surplus on Chip Exports
highlights.description: South Korea posted its biggest current account surplus on record in February as booming semiconductor exports lifted the goods balance before the Iran war began to ripple through energy markets and global shipping.
